title: rivina brasiliensis var. viridis
intro: Rivina brasiliensis var. viridis, also known as the red ivy or mirta palo de aceite, is a plant species native to South America, characterized by its bright red berries and green leaves. Traditionally, Rivina brasiliensis var. viridis has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rivina brasiliensis var. viridis include treating various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as using it as a natural remedy for wound healing. Herbal preparations of Rivina brasiliensis var. viridis include decoctions and infusions made from its roots, leaves, and berries, which can be used to treat a variety of health issues.
